perturbation.original_text: 8.1 The initial term of this Agreement shall, unless sooner terminated by consent of all parties, expires in five (5) years from the date of Effective Date. NVOS and HGF may renew the Agreement within two (2) years of the expiry of the initial term upon mutual understanding. 8.2 It is understood that a subsequent renewal of a five (5) year term will be negotiated in good faith and shall carry terms very close to the original Agreement. 8.3 Both parties may enter into buyout negotiations with the other Party on terms agreeable to both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 The initial term of this Agreement shall, unless sooner terminated by consent of all parties, expires in five (5) years from the date of Effective Date. NVOS, at its sole discretion, may renew the Agreement for an indefinite term. 8.2 It is understood that subsequent renewals will be subject to NVOS's internal review and approval, potentially altering terms significantly from the original Agreement. 8.3 Both parties may enter into buyout negotiations with the other Party on terms agreeable to both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement specifies a five-year term with mutual renewal. The modified version grants NVOS the sole discretion to renew for an indefinite term, contradicting the mutual understanding and good faith negotiation previously stipulated. This change causes uncertainty regarding the duration and terms of the agreement after the initial five-year period, favoring NVOS's terms, which contradicts the mutual agreement mentioned earlier.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 8 - TERM OF AGREEMENT 8.1 The initial term of this Agreement shall, unless sooner terminated by consent of all parties, expires in five (5) years from the date of Effective Date. NVOS, at its sole discretion, may renew the Agreement for an indefinite term. 8.2 It is understood that subsequent renewals will be subject to NVOS's internal review and approval, potentially altering terms significantly from the original Agreement. 8.3 Both parties may enter into buyout negotiations with the other Party on terms agreeable to both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original agreement states a five-year term with mutual renewal. The changed version grants NVOS sole discretion to renew for an indefinite term. This contradicts the original mutual understanding and good faith negotiation, causing uncertainty regarding the agreement's duration and terms after the initial period. The contradicted text, which includes the changed text, now reflects this contradiction, violating the initial intent of mutual agreement and creating a strong in-text contradiction as the changed text gives NVOS the sole discretion of renewal, while the original agreement required mutual understanding.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3 To remunerate HGF on the basis of thirty percent (30%) of net Company income basis on an annual basis commencing 12 months after the first full 12-month revenue period.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3 To remunerate HGF on the basis of thirty percent (30%) of net Company income basis on an annual basis commencing 12 months after the first full 12-month revenue period, unless NVOS determines that market conditions do not justify such payment.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that NVOS will pay HGF 30% of net company income. The added phrase 'unless NVOS determines that market conditions do not justify such payment' introduces an exception that undermines the obligation. This creates uncertainty about whether HGF will actually receive the agreed-upon percentage, as NVOS can unilaterally decide not to pay based on subjective 'market conditions.' This contradicts the originally stated commitment and introduces an imbalance in obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 9 - OBLIGATIONS OF NVOS 9.1 To maintain all financial records of the Company and provide quarterly and annual reporting to all Company stakeholders. All records are kept under US GAAP compliance standards. 9.2 Assign and direct operational staff from onset to agreement termination. 9.3 To remunerate HGF on the basis of thirty percent (30%) of net Company income basis on an annual basis commencing 12 months after the first full 12-month revenue period, unless NVOS determines that market conditions do not justify such payment. 9.4 To purchase product from the Company at a price of cost plus five percent (5%). 9.5 To issue two (2) million NVOS common stock upon successful target of twenty-five million dollars ($25M) of net profit achieved by the Company each fiscal year. NVOS common stock will be delivered to HGF via Novo Healthnet Limited ("NHL") exchangeable preferred shares. All parties understand NVOS is a U.S. reporting publicly traded corporation and that any NVOS common shares issued, from exchanging the NHL exchangeable preferred shares, will be provided under the guiding U.S. rules and regulations. Furthermore, all parties understand these shares will carry the same rights and conditions, with no special terms or conditions, as all NVOS common shares authorized for issue under the companies' Nevada Articles of Incorporation. Any NVOS common stock issued to HGF, on or after the date hereof, is subject to pro-rata adjustment in the event that NVOS shall, prior to the issuance date, approve any forward stock split, reverse stock split or other capitalization re-structure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('unless NVOS determines that market conditions do not justify such payment') to the obligation of NVOS to remunerate HGF with 30% of net company income, as stated in Article 9.3. This creates a direct conflict because it makes the payment conditional on NVOS's subjective assessment of 'market conditions,' contradicting the initial commitment to pay HGF that percentage. This undermines the enforceability of the remuneration clause.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 All staffing, including but not limited to, management, specialized or general labor requirements for farming will be the sole responsibility of HGF.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 All staffing, including but not limited to, management, specialized or general labor requirements for farming will be the shared responsibility of both NVOS and HGF, according to NVOS's final staffing decision.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, HGF was solely responsible for all staffing. By changing it to 'shared responsibility' but with NVOS having the 'final staffing decision', there is now an in-text contradiction. It is no longer clear who is responsible since the final staffing decision will come to NVOS, regardless of HGF having some sort of responsibility. HGF responsibilty is removed creating uncertainty of the parties responsibility and how it will be implemented.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 11 - MANAGEMENT PERSONNEL 11.1 All staffing, including but not limited to, management, specialized or general labor requirements for farming will be the shared responsibility of both NVOS and HGF, according to NVOS's final staffing decision.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the responsibility for staffing from solely HGF to a shared responsibility between NVOS and HGF, but grants NVOS the final decision. This directly contradicts the original allocation of responsibility, creating an inconsistency. The contradicted text reinforces this changed responsibility, but the altered control undermines HGF's responsibilities, causing uncertainty about the actual allocation of staffing duties.
